raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I used my Citi credit card to put a hold on a rental studio for a photo shoot for my business ( XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ). Upon physical inspection of the studio, it was not as advertised and was not suitable for the production. Myself and my producer let the studio owner know we would not be proceeding with the rental. The Studio owner in turn charged the full estimated amount of the rental on my card and refused to return the money ( {$6600.00} ). 

No cancellation policy was listed on the website. 
( They have since changed the website vaguely to say that deposits are not refundable ) There was no contract, only emails back and forth. 
No service was rendered, no rental of any space occurred. 

I tried multiple times to work with Citi to deny the charge as it was fraudulent, and had nothing to do with any goods or services rendered ; However Citi made it impossible to speak to anyone or gain any resolution. Furthermore, Citi only allowed me to speak to people who barely spoke XXXX and couldn't even provide the most basic of assistance or help, they routinely got facts and information incorrect due to a lack of XXXX proficiency. 

It is my opinion that Citi went to great lengths to stonewall my efforts to deny charges from a merchant who knowingly committed fraud. I have been going back and forth with this so long due to the COVID-19 epidemic and I'm desperate for the money just to be returned and to be done with this saga.
